questions.question: What is the difference between internal and external commands in UNIX?
options: Internal commands are started by typing a command and press enter, while external commands are started by typing the command followed by a space and then press enter.
The internal commands are stored in the shell and can be accessed directly, while external commands are stored in the disk and need to be accessed first.
Internal commands are started with a space and external commands are started with a tab
Internal commands are executed faster than external commands.
Internal commands are started by typing a command and press enter, while external commands are started by typing the command followed by a space and then press enter.
questions.explanation: Internal commands are stored in the shell and can be accessed directly, while external commands are stored in the disk and need to be accessed first.

questions.question: Can internal commands be modified?
options: Yes, internal commands can be modified.
No, internal commands cannot be modified.
Internal commands are always executed from the shell.
External commands are executed faster than internal commands.
questions.explanation: Internal commands are always stored in the shell and cannot be modified.

questions.question: What is the purpose of external commands?
options: To perform complex tasks and operations.
To execute a single command and perform the desired operation.
To provide a way to external programs to interact with the shell.
To create new shells.
questions.explanation: External commands provide a way to external programs to interact with the shell.

questions.question: Can external commands be accessed directly?
options: No, external commands cannot be accessed directly.
Yes, external commands can be accessed directly.
External commands are always stored in the disk.
Internal commands are executed faster than external commands.
questions.explanation: External commands can be accessed directly by using their names followed by a space and then press enter.

questions.question: Are internal commands and external commands mutually exclusive?
options: Yes, internal commands and external commands are mutually exclusive.
No, internal commands and external commands are not mutually exclusive.
Internal commands are a type of external command.
External commands are a type of internal command.
questions.explanation: Internal commands and external commands are not mutually exclusive, both types of commands can be used together.
